name: n8n update
description: Automated workflow: n8n update. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, telegram, scheduleTrigger, merge. It contains 32 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

parameters.content:
## Default Variables  
Before starting, please set the following variables:  

- **working-directory**: The directory where your `docker-compose.yml` file is located.  
- **n8n-container-name**: The name of your n8n Docker container.  
- **telegram-id**: Your Telegram chat ID. You can find it by messaging `@get_id_bot` on Telegram.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content: Get information from the n8n GitHub repository to find the latest released version of n8n.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
This node removes "n8n@" from the version string.  
For example:  
- Before: `n8n@1.84.3`  
- After: `1.84.3`

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## Check Installed Image Version  
Execute a command on the server to determine which version of n8n is currently running.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## SQL Query: Merging Inputs  
This query retrieves the `stdout` field from `input1` and `tag_name` from `input2`.  
It uses a `LEFT JOIN` to merge the data based on matching `id` fields.  

Query:  
```sql
SELECT input1.stdout AS stdout, 
input2.tag_name AS tag_name
FROM input1
LEFT JOIN input2 ON input1.id = input2.id;

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

## Telegram Notificaton [OPTIONAL]
Send a Telegram message and inform that there is nothing to do and n8n is already on the latest version.

parameters.content:
## Compare Versions  
This node compares two versions: one from Docker and another from the n8n GitHub repository.  
If a new version is available, it will be detected.

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: n8n update. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, telegram, scheduleTrigger, merge. It contains 32 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: n8n update. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content:
## Default Variables  
Before starting, please set the following variables:  

- **working-directory**: The directory where your `docker-compose.yml` file is located.  
- **n8n-container-name**: The name of your n8n Docker container.  
- **telegram-id**: Your Telegram chat ID. You can find it by messaging `@get_id_bot` on Telegram.
Get information from the n8n GitHub repository to find the latest released version of n8n.

## Docker Compose Up 
In this step, the container will be started with the new image.

## Telegram Notificaton
Send a Telegram notification and inform that n8n has been updated.

## Schedule Trigger  
You can schedule this workflow, for example, every three days to check n8n images.

This node removes "n8n@" from the version string.  
For example:  
- Before: `n8n@1.84.3`  
- After: `1.84.3`

## Check Installed Image Version  
Execute a command on the server to determine which version of n8n is currently running.

## SQL Query: Merging Inputs  
This query retrieves the `stdout` field from `input1` and `tag_name` from `input2`.  
It uses a `LEFT JOIN` to merge the data based on matching `id` fields.  

Query:  
```sql
SELECT input1.stdout AS stdout, 
input2.tag_name AS tag_name
FROM input1
LEFT JOIN input2 ON input1.id = input2.id;

## Telegram Notificaton [OPTIONAL]
Send a Telegram message and inform that there is nothing to do and n8n is already on the latest version.

## Compare Versions  
This node compares two versions: one from Docker and another from the n8n GitHub repository.  
If a new version is available, it will be detected.

## Telegram Approve
Send a Telegram notification and inform that a new version is available. Ask if the user wants to update.

## Pull Docker Image
Pull the latest n8n image. You can modify the command as needed.

## Docker Compose Pull
Runs in the directory you defined in the Default variable.

parameters.message:
Hi, 
a new n8n version is available. 
I'm ready to update. 
Can I start now?

parameters.text: We are updating n8n to the latest version.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Pull n8n Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.ssh) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "docker compose pull" node (n8n-nodes-base.ssh) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "check n8n installed version" node (n8n-nodes-base.ssh) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note10" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note11" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "docker compose up" node (n8n-nodes-base.ssh) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note12" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Github HTTP Request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge Results" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Comapre Two Input" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Notif" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Approve" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Notif1" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Default variable".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Version String".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
